executive_summary.overall_assessment: The telemetry and safety implementations demonstrate strong architectural alignment with spec.md requirements, but critical gaps exist in HUD telemetry output and complete pipeline integration.
critical_gaps: AM7-L2-HUD-004: Missing 120Hz HUD telemetry socket output (ICD-006)
AM7-L2-ACT-001: Fusion HAT+ I2C gimbal interface missing
compliant_areas: AM7-L2-SAFE-007: Safety monitor with software watchdog implemented
AM7-L3-SAFE-005: Software watchdog with 50ms kick interval implemented
executive_summary.recommendation: BLOCK progression to implementation phase until critical gaps addressed. Telemetry writer requires HUD socket extension. Safety monitor requires integration with missing subsystems.

findings.issue_description: TelemetryWriter implements CSV logging (ICD-004) but lacks HUD telemetry socket output required by AM7-L2-HUD-004 and ICD-006. Spec requires 120Hz telemetry via UNIX domain socket at /run/aurore/hud_telemetry.sock.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: ICD-006 requires UNIX domain socket (SOCK_SEQPACKET) at /run/aurore/hud_telemetry.sock with 64-byte messages containing sync_word, message_id, sequence, timestamp, payload, and HMAC. Update rate must be 120Hz synchronized to frame boundary.

security_considerations: ICD-006 requires HMAC-SHA256 authentication over message header + payload
Socket permissions must be 0660, owner aurore:operator
Stale telemetry (>100ms) must trigger HUD warning indicator
alternatives: Shared memory ring buffer (8KB) as alternative to UNIX socket per ICD-006

findings.issue_description: ThreadTiming uses CLOCK_MONOTONIC for sleep operations (line 147-152) but spec AM7-L3-TIM-001 requires CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW as sole time source for all timing-critical operations. Implementation partially compliant: uses CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW for timestamps but CLOCK_MONOTONIC for clock_nanosleep.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: Linux kernel does not support CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW with clock_nanosleep(). The implementation correctly uses CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW for timestamp capture (get_timestamp) and CLOCK_MONOTONIC for sleep operations. This is a known kernel limitation, not a compliance violation. Verification method in AM7-L3-TIM-001 targets absence of gettimeofday()/time()/rdtsc, which is satisfied.

alternatives: Use timer_create(CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW) + sigwait() for absolute timing (complex)
Accept CLOCK_MONOTONIC for sleep with documented justification

findings.issue_description: SafetyMonitor implements software watchdog per AM7-L3-SAFE-005 but lacks integration with hardware interlock. Spec AM7-L3-SAFE-001 requires interlock default to inhibit on CPU reset, power loss, or safety fault via hardware pull-down resistor. Current implementation is software-only.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: AM7-L3-SAFE-001 requires interlock default to inhibit via hardware pull-down resistor (10kΩ) independent of software. Software fault handling must command interlock to inhibit state. Requires Fusion HAT+ I2C interface (missing per DRIFT_004).

security_considerations: Hardware pull-down resistor (10kΩ) required for fail-safe default
I2C communication failure must trigger inhibit
Interlock state must be logged in audit trail per AM7-L2-SEC-003

findings.issue_description: TelemetryFrame lacks queue_overflow_count field recommended by general-purpose_diagnostics_001.json. Mk VI analysis showed queue overflow tracking was critical for debugging pipeline stalls. Current implementation only tracks current queue_depth.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: Queue overflow tracking enables identification of backpressure bottlenecks. Mk VI analysis showed this was essential for debugging pipeline stalls. Implementation requires ~20 LOC addition to TelemetryFrame and CSV output.

findings.issue_description: TelemetryWriter::log_event exists but lacks hardware failure event logging methods recommended by general-purpose_diagnostics_001.json. Mk VI's log_camera_failure(), log_tpu_unavailable(), log_actuation_error() were critical for production debugging.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: Hardware failure event logging enables debugging of production issues where camera/TPU/I2C failed. Mk VI showed these events were critical for understanding system behavior under fault conditions.

findings.issue_description: Per documentation-drift-detector_diagnostics_001.json DRIFT_002, seven-state machine (BOOT, IDLE/SAFE, FREECAM, SEARCH, TRACKING, ARMED, FAULT) is completely missing. This violates AM7-L1-MODE-002 and prevents safety state enforcement. Current main.cpp has TODO stub.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: AM7-L1-MODE-002 requires seven mandatory states. AM7-L2-MODE-007 requires explicit transitions only (no spontaneous/implicit). ARMED only from TRACKING. FAULT from any state. FAULT->BOOT requires power cycle. Implementation requires ~400-600 LOC.

security_considerations: ARMED state only accessible from TRACKING per AM7-L2-MODE-006
Any fault must transition to FAULT per AM7-L2-MODE-007
FAULT state forces interlock inhibit per AM7-L3-SAFE-001

findings.issue_description: Per documentation-drift-detector_diagnostics_001.json DRIFT_001, CSRT tracker implementation is completely missing. Spec AM7-L2-VIS-008 requires CSRT (Channel Spatial Reliability Tracker) for continuous target tracking at 120Hz at 1536×864 resolution.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: AM7-L2-VIS-008 requires CSRT tracker at 120Hz. AM7-L2-VIS-009 requires hardware acceleration (ARM NEON SIMD and/or VideoCore VII GPU). OpenCV provides cv::TrackerCSRT implementation. Requires ~200-300 LOC for full integration with motion prediction.

deprecation_warnings: OpenCV CSRT tracker may have performance issues at 1536×864@120Hz without GPU acceleration

findings.issue_description: Per documentation-drift-detector_diagnostics_001.json DRIFT_003, ballistic engine is completely missing. Spec AM7-L2-BALL-001 through AM7-L2-BALL-004 require ballistic solution calculation based on configurable ammunition profiles within 1ms budget.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: AM7-L2-BALL-001 requires ballistic engine. AM7-L2-BALL-002 requires config.json profiles. AM7-L2-BALL-003 requires aim point calculation. AM7-L2-BALL-004 requires ≤1ms timing. Implementation requires ~150-200 LOC.

security_considerations: Ballistic solution accuracy critical for safety
Profiles must be validated before use
Solution must be revoked if range data stale per AM7-L3-SAFE-002

findings.issue_description: Per documentation-drift-detector_diagnostics_001.json DRIFT_004, Fusion HAT+ I2C interface is missing. Spec AM7-L2-ACT-001 through AM7-L2-ACT-003 require gimbal command updates at 120Hz via I2C to GD32 MCU at address 0x17.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: ICD-002 specifies I2C protocol to GD32 MCU at 0x17. PWM channels 0-1 for gimbal (elevation, azimuth). Channel 2 for interlock. Update rate 120Hz ±1%. Latency ≤2ms. Implementation requires ~100-150 LOC.

security_considerations: I2C NACK must trigger retry (3x) then fault
I2C timeout (>10ms) must trigger safety fault
Commands must be authenticated per AM7-L2-SEC-001

findings.issue_description: TelemetryWriter lacks SharedMetrics integration for real-time TUI monitoring. general-purpose_diagnostics_001.json identified this as high-priority feature from Mk VI. Current implementation only writes CSV/JSON files.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: SharedMetrics enables real-time TUI/dashboard without CSV parsing overhead. Mk VI used this for live monitoring. Implementation requires ~80 LOC for SharedMetrics struct plus integration.

correct_usage_pattern.description: Complete telemetry and safety implementation per spec.md v5.0 requirements

known_issues: CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW not supported by clock_nanosleep() - use CLOCK_MONOTONIC for sleep
OpenCV CSRT tracker may not achieve 120Hz at 1536×864 without GPU acceleration
I2C communication to Fusion HAT+ requires libi2c-dev package

requirements_verified.description: System shall use CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW as sole time source
requirements_verified.evidence: timing.hpp uses CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW for get_timestamp(). clock_nanosleep uses CLOCK_MONOTONIC (kernel limitation, not violation).

requirements_verified.description: Critical threads shall use SCHED_FIFO with specified priorities
requirements_verified.evidence: main.cpp configures SCHED_FIFO priorities: vision_pipeline=90, track_compute=85, actuation_output=95

requirements_verified.description: System shall lock all memory pages (mlockall)
Safety function self-test at power-on and every 100ms
requirements_verified.evidence: SafetyMonitor implements software watchdog with 50ms kick interval, 60ms timeout

requirements_verified.description: Fault register latched, non-clearable except via power cycle
requirements_verified.evidence: SafetyMonitor::trigger_emergency_stop sets emergency_active, but clear_fault can clear non-emergency faults

requirements_verified.description: HUD telemetry update rate 120Hz synchronized to frame boundary
requirements_verified.evidence: TelemetryWriter lacks HUD socket implementation. ICD-006 interface missing.
src/tracking/ directory empty. No CSRT implementation.
src/state_machine/ directory empty. No state machine implementation.

requirements_verified.description: Ballistic engine for aim point calculation
requirements_verified.evidence: src/actuation/ directory empty. No ballistic solver.

requirements_verified.description: Gimbal command updates at 120Hz via I2C
Logger interface with ring buffer, CSV output
requirements_verified.evidence: TelemetryWriter implements CSV logging with buffered writes, session management

findings: Missing integration with hardware interlock (requires FusionHAT)
ThreadTiming uses CLOCK_MONOTONIC for sleep (kernel limitation)

missing_requirements.impact: Cannot provide telemetry to remote operator interface
Cannot track targets, system cannot enter TRACKING/ARMED states
No safety state enforcement, no mode transitions
Cannot calculate ballistic solution for HUD

undocumented_features.justification: Linux kernel does not support CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW with clock_nanosleep(). Document this limitation in timing.hpp comments.

recommendations.description: Implement seven-state machine before any pipeline integration
recommendations.rationale: State machine is fundamental to safety architecture. Without it, no mode transitions, safety state enforcement, or operator control is possible.

recommendations.description: Implement CSRT tracker for target tracking
recommendations.rationale: CSRT is required for TRACKING state. Without target tracking, system cannot transition to ARMED state.

recommendations.description: Implement Fusion HAT+ I2C interface for gimbal/interlock control
recommendations.rationale: Required for actuation output and safety interlock. Without I2C interface, no gimbal control or interlock enforcement.

recommendations.description: Add HUD telemetry socket to TelemetryWriter
recommendations.rationale: Required for remote operator interface. ICD-006 specifies 120Hz telemetry via UNIX socket.
Required for aim point calculation and HUD reticle lead indication.

recommendations.description: Add queue overflow tracking to TelemetryFrame
recommendations.rationale: Mk VI analysis showed this was critical for debugging pipeline stalls. Low effort, high value.

recommendations.description: Add SharedMetrics integration for real-time monitoring
recommendations.rationale: Enables TUI dashboard without CSV parsing. Mk VI showed this was valuable for live monitoring.

recommendations.description: Add hardware failure event logging methods
recommendations.rationale: Convenience methods for common failures (camera, actuation, I2C). Mk VI showed these were useful for debugging.
Clarify why clock_nanosleep uses CLOCK_MONOTONIC instead of CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW (kernel limitation).

compliance_rationale: Compliance is NOT complete. Critical gaps exist in: (1) HUD telemetry socket output (AM7-L2-HUD-004), (2) CSRT tracker (AM7-L2-VIS-008), (3) seven-state machine (AM7-L1-MODE-002), (4) ballistic engine (AM7-L2-BALL-001), (5) Fusion HAT+ I2C interface (AM7-L2-ACT-001). These gaps block progression to implementation phase. Telemetry writer and safety monitor implementations are architecturally sound but require integration with missing subsystems. Recommendation: BLOCK implementation phase until REC_001 (state machine) and REC_004 (HUD socket) completed. Other gaps can be addressed in parallel with implementation phase.
